sanskrit:
प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वशः।
यः पश्यति तथात्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति॥
transliteration:
prakåtyaiva ca karmäëi kriyamäëäni sarvaçaù
yaù paçyati tathätmänam akartäraà sa paçyati
literal_meaning: All actions are being performed everywhere by prakåti (material nature); one who sees the Self as the non-doer—he truly sees.
contextual_meaning: The mind-body system runs on autopilot shaped by genetics, upbringing, and unconscious drives; recognizing that the observing Self is not the compulsive actor dissolves neurotic ownership of thoughts and behaviors.
psychological_state: Observer stance; reduced self-blame and rumination; metacognitive clarity.
trigger_scenario: Snapping at your child after work, then realizing the outburst was an automatic stress reaction rather than a personal failure.
anchor_text: Watch life unfold; you are the silent witness, not the programmed doer.
